title: Coachella 2026: Justin Bieber launches a major comeback in the desert
subtitle: N/A
content: Coachella 2026: Justin Bieber launches a major comeback in the desert | Coachella | The Guardian Skip to main content Skip to navigation Skip to navigation Justin Bieber performs onstage. Photograph: John Shearer/Getty Images for The Recording Academy View image in fullscreen Justin Bieber performs onstage. Photograph: John Shearer/Getty Images for The Recording Academy Coachella 2026: Justin Bieber launches a major comeback in the desert Stars including Sabrina Carpenter, Karol G, David Byrne and Addison Rae also head to the desert for the first of two sold-out weekends of live music Justin Bieber is set for a major live performance comeback at this year’s sold-out Coachella with rainy weather set to be a possible spoiler. The Canadian singer will face his biggest live stage since he abandoned his 2022 tour over health concerns. Bieber was experiencing “full paralysis” on one side of his face after being diagnosed with Ramsay Hunt syndrome. “I wish this wasn’t the case but obviously my body is telling me I gotta slow down,” he said at the time. Last year, he said the idea of touring again was “super daunting” to him and he was not planning to put on an official tour anytime soon. “I’m putting on a hell of a show for you guys for Coachella , getting ready, and getting inspired,” he also said to fans on Twitch. The annual music festival, which takes place in California’s Coachella valley, will host about 125,000 music lovers a day with seven stages offering a variety of artists including fellow headliners Sabrina Carpenter and Karol G. Other artists will include Moby, Geese, David Byrne, Addison Rae, the Strokes, Sombr, Iggy Pop, Ethel Cain and Labrinth over two weekends. Tickets sold out in just three days, an encouraging sign of fan interest for the second year in a row following 2024’s edition suffering from the slowest sales to date. View image in fullscreen Coachella in 2025. Photograph: Amy Harris/Invision/AP Coachella is also set to host a comeback for The xx , performing their first major festival after eight years ahead of their first album since 2017. Last week saw a surprise performance in Mexico City, announced as “the start of a next chapter together”. Carpenter, who performed at Coachella in 2024, has promised that her headlining set will be the “most ambitious” show she has ever done. “It’s probably the most time I’ve ever had to actually just sit down and talk about a show as I’m building it,” she said in an interview with Marc Jacobs this week. On Sunday, Karol G, the Colombian singer, will become Coachella’s first ever Latina headliner. She recently spoke about how she has been warned not to make any anti-ICE statements over fears it might affect her visa. “You become bait, because some people want to show their power,” she said in a Playboy interview. She also added that she is “willing to say” ICE out but “it’s something that crosses the line of what I have to do to protect myself”. This week’s schedule saw the surprise addition of Jack White while no-shows will include the punk duo Lambrini Girls after guitarist Phoebe Lunny announced on Instagram that she has “an acute brain injury”. Girl group Katseye member Manon will also miss the festival as she remains on a hiatus to “focus on her health and well-being”. After last year saw near-record temperatures, rising up to 102F (39C), which led to a rise in heat-related visits to nearby hospitals, this weekend sees the possibility of rain. Earlier reports of a potential thunderstorm have since been downgraded but weather will still see a drastic shift from 2025 highs. title: Justin Bieber's best — and most infamous — Coachella Valley headlines
subtitle: N/A
content: Justin Bieber loves Palm Springs. But it doesn't always love him back Skip to main content Home Sports Business News Life Opinion Obituaries Desert Magazine eNewspaper Archives Crosswords Comics From The Vault Newsletters Desert Scene & Giving For Subscribers Shopping Contributor Content Justin Bieber's best — and most infamous — Coachella Valley headlines Justin Bieber's best — and most infamous — Coachella Valley headlines Justin Bieber has a complicated history with the desert, from philanthropic moments to clashes with paparazzi. Let's take a walk down memory lane. Ema Sasic Palm Springs Desert Sun Show Caption Hide Caption Coachella billboards are back on I-10. How many can you spot? Artist billboards have made their way onto Interstate 10 east and west ahead of the Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival. If there's one performer on the 2026 Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival lineup that comes with a bit of baggage, it's Justin Bieber. Now, there's no denying that the Grammy Award winner is talented — he impressed us with his stripped-down performance of "Yukon" in February and it made us even more excited for his headlining sets on Saturday, April 11 and 18. But sometimes musical savants don't always make the best decisions off-stage, and Bieber has had a few of those bad moments in the Coachella Valley. The singer frequents the area quite a bit — he and his wife, Hailey, have a home in La Quinta's exclusive Madison Club — and, as a result, he's had a few less-than-stellar moments as a resident, including clashes with paparazzi. But he's also shown his charitable side and given fans plenty to scream about during his many Coachella sightings. Without further ado, here are some of Bieber's good, bad and ugly moments in the Coachella Valley over the years. More: Did the Justin Bieber Grammys set give us clues for Coachella 2026? Justin Bieber clashes with paparazzi ahead of Coachella 2025 Bieber was spotted ahead of the 2025 Coachella festival outside of Vintage Coffee House in Indio, a desert spot he has frequented over the years (yay supporting local businesses!). Unfortunately, things turned sour when he got in a verbal altercation with some paparazzi filming him approaching and walking into the business, according to TMZ. In the video posted by the outlet, the singer approached the business with a small group of friends, and when a photographer said, "Good morning," Bieber appeared agitated and said "No, not good morning." He asked them why they were there and said they only wanted money. "You don't care about people, only money, no people, you don't care about human beings," he said while walking toward one of the photographers, rubbing his fingers together in a money gesture. He also repeatedly told the paps who were filming him to leave the area. Is it annoying to constantly have to deal with paparazzi everywhere you go? Absolutely. But he pretty much gave them the outraged reaction they were looking for, as the altercation was picked up by various outlets and tabloids. Oh, the life of a famous person; they just can't seem to win. Justin Bieber caught urinating on Palm Springs golf course More: Billie Eilish? Gunna? Here's who could join Justin Bieber at Coachella Duty calls wherever you may be, including a golf course. In November 2025, the singer was caught urinating while playing a round of golf in Palm Springs. Photographs posted on PageSix showed the "Sorry" singer facing a line of bushes on the course and taking care of business, later pulling up his baggy shorts when he was finished. Public urination is considered a misdemeanor in California and can result in up to $1,000 in fines and/or up to six months in jail. PageSix could not confirm if the singer was on a public golf course when the incident occurred or if he received any fines. We can slightly turn our heads the other way (literally) for this one as urinating on golf courses has a bit of a history with the sport. If you don't take my word for it, here's what touring pro Kevin Na told Golf Digest in 2014: "I guarantee you, everyone has a story," he said. "We've all gone out and found a tree or a bush at some point." We'll let it slide this time, Bieber, but if there's a restroom available on the grounds, we suggest taking advantage of it. Justin Bieber makes Billie Eilish's dream come true Bieber has been a longtime fixture at Coachella, being spotted nearly every year in the crowd and having the time of his life. He's also surprised fans at least four times in the past as a special guest for artists such as Chance the Rapper, Ariana Grande, Daniel Caesar and Tems. One person whose life he truly changed was Billie Eilish when they met for the first time in 2019 following her flawed festival debut . As seen in viral social media videos and Eilish's documentary "Billie Eilish: The World's a Little Blurry," the "Peaches" singer went right up to her in a VIP section. Eilish, a self-proclaimed Bieber superfan, initially hid from him. After he kept looking at her and she looked back in disbelief, they finally embraced and didn't let go of each other for quite some time. Fans surrounding them in the barricades even cheered when they went in for a hug. It's a wholesome moment that'll make anyone smile and remind us that even celebrities get starstruck when meeting their heroes for the first time. Who knows, maybe Bieber will bring out Eilish as a surprise guest during his headlining set for a duet? Now that would be a moment not to forget. Hailey Bieber suffers health scare, Justin Bieber springs into action More: Sabrina Carpenter, Justin Bieber Coachella billboards spotted on I-10 This one involves Bieber's other half, Hailey, who, unfortunately, had quite a health scare while in the Coachella Valley in March 2022 . In a 12-minute video posted on her YouTube channel, Hailey shared that she was eating breakfast with her husband and having a "normal day and normal conversation." Suddenly, she felt "this really weird sensation that traveled down my arm from my shoulder all the way down to my fingertips," which made them feel numb. When Bieber asked her if she was OK, she said she couldn't speak. "The right side of my face started drooping," Hailey said. "I couldn't get a sentence out." She was taken to Eisenhower Health in Rancho Mirage, and after a series of scans, health care workers discovered she suffered a small blood clot in her brain that was labeled and categorized as a transient ischemic attack. It is a temporary period of symptoms similar to those of a stroke, according to the Mayo Clinic . A TIA usually lasts a few minutes and doesn't cause permanent damage, but about one in three people who have had a TIA will eventually have a stroke. About half occur within one year of the "ministroke." We can only imagine how scary that situation must have been for the both of them, but Bieber leaped into action by getting her the medical attention she needed immediately. That's exactly the type of person you want to have by your side when seconds count. Justin Bieber raises funds for charity at Desert Smash More: Coachella is almost here. So are ticket scams. How to avoid them Aside from Coachella, Bieber has shown a lot of love toward the desert's tennis scene, particularly the Desert Smash charity event . Back in 2015, the singer teamed up with comedians Will Ferrell and Kevin Hart to raise money for Cancer for College, a charity founded by Craig Pollard, Ferrell's USC fraternity brother. Hart tried to keep the mood light and fun, often making jokes at Bieber's expense, such as teasing him for needing a water break. Bieber also crooned for the crowd at an intimate acoustic set later, showing everyone his vocal chops and making everyone wish they were his "Baby." All in a day's work for a superstar. Other unforgettable Bieber moments in the desert This one technically indirectly involved him, but is too funny not to share. In 2014, a sign referencing Bieber posted in the window of the Michael Weems' Autoerotica Gallery at North Palm Canyon Drive in Palm Springs turned heads — and apparently caused a stir among local Canadians — because the message read "Canadians, take Bieber with you." TMZ reported that Bieber was escorted out of Coachella 2015 after being put in a choke hold post-attempting to go back stage with Drake. A grainy and inconclusive video of the incident was also shared online by the celebrity gossip website. Right after his big 22nd birthday bash in Los Angeles in 2016, Bieber traveled to the desert for a getaway at the Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage, where he posed with a breastfeeding mother and was spotted at a nearby McDonalds. During the weekend of Coachella 2018, TMZ reported that the pop star punched a man who was choking a woman at a party in La Quinta. Bieber appeared to come to the rescue by hitting the man and pushing him against a wall, allowing the woman to be freed from the man’s grasp. Ema Sasic covers entertainment and health in the Coachella Valley. Reach her at ema.sasic@desertsun.com or on Twitter @ema_sasic. title: Justin Bieber Coachella Rehearsal Caught on Camera, See the Sneak Peek
subtitle: N/A
content: Justin Bieber Coachella Rehearsal Caught on Camera, See the Sneak Peek Comic's Set Trashing Keegan-Michael Key's Wife Is 'Outlandish, Fabricated,' Sources Say Chix On Netflix -- Guess Whose 'Selling' Hot Shots?! 'Summer House' West Wilson's Alleged Murderer Cousin Dakota Could Face Mental Exam Guess The Future Beauty Pageant Queen This Girl Turned Into! 'White Lotus' Sam Nivola Says Helena Bonham Carter's Sudden Exit Not Shocking Getty Justin Bieber 's already in the desert prepping for Coachella ... and some of his rehearsal was caught on camera. TikTok users are posting videos that seem to show Biebs practicing for his big headlining set this weekend. You can't see much ... but it sounds like you can hear the pop star singing along to his backing tracks. **Potential Coachella Spoilers below** TikTok Post Unavailable In one clip, JB is running through hits like "Sorry" and "Where Are Ü Now" -- which could open things up for possible cameos by Diplo and Skrillex . And in a second clip ... Justin's heard practicing "YUKON," the second single off his recent album, "SWAG." TikTok Post Unavailable Another video shows a fan filming their reaction to hearing the rehearsal ... naming each song as the singer ran through them. Of the ones caught on camera, you can hear major throwbacks like "Baby," "Favorite Girl," "One Less Lonely Girl" and "All That Matters." TikTok Post Unavailable That clip also teases covers of "With You" by Chris Brown and "So Sick" by Ne-Yo -- two songs JB had covered on YouTube as a child. Bieber's been gearing up to take the stage in Indio, California by playing a couple of intimate shows in L.A. recently -- where he reportedly only played songs from "SWAG" and "SWAG II." But if these videos are any indication of what he'll play during his Coachella sets ... then he doesn't plan to completely forego the hits! title: Justin Bieber is a Coachella regular. Now's the right time to headline
subtitle: N/A
content: What time is Justin Bieber performing at Coachella? Skip to main content Home Sports Business News Life Opinion Obituaries Desert Magazine eNewspaper Archives Crosswords Comics From The Vault Newsletters Desert Scene & Giving For Subscribers Shopping Contributor Content Justin Bieber is a Coachella regular. Now's the right time to headline Justin Bieber is a Coachella regular. Now's the right time to headline Ema Sasic Palm Springs Desert Sun Show Caption Hide Caption Ready for music festival season? What to know about Coachella 2026 Headed to the Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival 2026, or just planning to watch from home? Here's everything to know about this year's fest. As the start of Coachella 2026 draws near, many attendees are eagerly anticipating the return of one artist: Justin Bieber. When the pop star was announced as a headliner back in September, it drew a number of reactions. For many, it was thrilling because he's never been a billed performer on the lineup, previously only showing up as an attendee or special guest. It's also hard to believe it's his first Coachella set considering he was everywhere in the 2010s with hits such as "Baby," "Sorry" and Love Yourself." But this seems like the right time — 11:25 p.m. on Saturday, April 11, to be exact — for the singer to take the stage. More: Justin Bieber's best — and most infamous — Coachella Valley headlines After several years of constant media attention, Bieber recently took time away from the stage due to his health. That break, in turn, proved to be productive as he released "Swag" and "Swag II" last year, giving us an intimate look into his domestic life and how his musicality has matured since his teenage years. Bieber's headlining performance has the potential to be one of the most talked about and memorable sets from not just this festival, but of the decade. There's still much to wonder about his set, but some recent live appearances have given us some clues for what to expect. Bieber will take the Coachella stage Saturday, April 11, with the first weekend of the festival taking place Friday, April 10 through Sunday, April 12 at the Empire Polo Club in Indio. Past Coachella appearances draw excitement Though the 2026 festival marks the first time Bieber has been on the lineup, it's not the first time he's performed at Coachella. More: Billie Eilish? Gunna? Here's who could join Justin Bieber at Coachella Some of the artists he's guested with include Chance the Rapper in 2014 for the song "Confident"; 2019 headliner Ariana Grande to sing his hit "Sorry"; Daniel Caesar and Giveon in 2022 for a performance of "Peaches"; and Tems and Wizkid for the song "Essence." Each time he's appeared, the crowd's love and passion for him has been clear. Cheers and screams can be heard throughout the Empire Polo Club, signaling a superstar is in people's midst. Not to mention, Bieber's vocal chops only continue to get stronger and more soulful. Aside from on-stage appearances, Bieber has been a staple among the Coachella grounds. Fans have spotted him dancing with his wife, Hailey, hanging out with friends and even making Billie Eilish's dreams come true by meeting in person. However, it's also been reported that he punched a man in 2018 for choking a woman at an after party in La Quinta, so you never really know what you'll get with him. Why Coachella 2026 is the right time for Justin Bieber to headline Some fans might think Coachella organizers missed an opportunity in the 2010s to have Bieber on its lineup. "Bieber Fever" was all the rage then, and he consistently came out with chart-topping songs. But I reckon the Bieber we see on stage Saturday knows more about himself, the music he wants to put out and the show he wants to deliver than the Bieber we would have gotten a decade ago. The 2020s have seen a lot of change in Bieber's life. He and Hailey became parents in 2024, welcoming a son, and that has had a profound impact on the singer's personal life and work. It can be heard in his albums "Swag" and "Swag II," in which he explores married life and his journey into fatherhood. "When you take my hand, honey, I promise you just tenderness/Pull me out when you come in, and you turn me to a better man," he sings on "Better Man." In the song "Mother in You," Bieber croons about seeing so much of himself and his wife in their child with the lyrics, "My smile, her grace, both of our hearts." Those records have also shown the way he's matured when it comes to his music. Long gone are the ultra-poppy songs from his breakout years. Instead, listeners now get R&B and indie sounds with smooth vocals and infectious beats that paint vivid images. The 32-year-old has also been open about health struggles, addiction and the burden of fame over the years. At times, we've seen his more challenging moments — like when he clashed with paparazzi in Indio last year — but it's also humanized this figure who has been in the spotlight for nearly two decades now. What to expect from Justin Bieber's headlining set The big question remains: What is Bieber going to do when he hits the stage on Saturday? While we won't know for sure until his set begins, we've gotten some clues in recent weeks. The singer took the Grammy Awards stage in February — four years after his last performance of "Peaches" at the award show — and delivered a stripped-down version of "Yukon" from "Swag." He stepped out in lavender boxer shorts, black socks and a guitar, and while many artists opted for flashy production design, he was alone on stage with his music production equipment. Utilizing a looping station, Bieber built up the beat for his song before he let his soulful vocals take center stage. Throughout the performance, the singer kept his eyes closed and his hands close to his body, not at all acknowledging the crowd in front of him. It was a great reintroduction to the singer, who has taken a lengthy hiatus from performing after being diagnosed with Ramsay Hunt syndrome , which can cause facial weakness/paralysis. The Grammys set also showed that he doesn't need much to bring in an audience. Whenever the camera panned out into the crowd, people were captivated by the music and vibe he set. There's also the question of whether he'll stick to just his most recent work, or if he'll do a whole retrospective performance with some of his earlier hits at Coachella. In recent invite-only Los Angeles shows at The Roxy and Troubadour venues, the singer has opted for "Swag" and "Swag II" tracks, which leads many to believe they might not hear "Baby" after all. It's worth noting that Bieber sold his publishing and recorded music rights in 2022 for more than $200 million. The deal included his entire back catalog, totaling 290 songs released before Dec. 31, 2021. It's not clear if the sale would bar the singer from performing any of those songs, but we have a feeling he'd be allowed to sing them (it is Coachella, after all). Though there's many questions and speculation surrounding this performance, there's one thing we know to be true: Tens of thousands of festivalgoers will gather around the Coachella Stage to take in this decades-in-the-making performance. What day is Justin Bieber performing at Coachella? Saturday, April 11 and Saturday, April 18. Only Weekend 1 set times have been released so far, and for Weekend 1, his set will begin at 11:25 p.m. Coachella 2026 lineup Want to see who else is on this year's festival lineup? See the full list here. (This story was updated to add a link.) Ema Sasic covers entertainment and health in the Coachella Valley. Reach her at ema.sasic@desertsun.com or on Twitter @ema_sasic. title: Justin Bieber Is Ready for Big Comeback with Coachella Headlining Performance
subtitle: N/A
content: Justin Bieber Is Ready for Big Comeback with Coachella Headlining Performance Skip to Main Content Getty Images Justin Bieber is ready for a headlining performance at Coachella! The Biebs prepared with two top-secret stops at iconic L.A. clubs the Roxy and the Troubadour for very small and lucky crowds. These were Bieber’s first solo shows in four years. Coachella reportedly paid him a cool $10 million for just two nights! It’s being called the beginning of his comeback, and there were even reports he’s recording his performance for a Netflix documentary. Netflix told “Extra” those headlines are not true. Sabrina Carpenter also returns to the desert, as a headliner this time, with rumors that Madonna may join her on the 17th to perform their as-yet-unheard new duet! Plus, international superstar Karol G will make Coachella history as the first-ever Latina to headline. title: Justin Bieber Fans Lose It Over Leaked Coachella Rehearsals, Tickets Hit $4K
subtitle: N/A
content: Justin Bieber Fans Lose It Over Leaked Coachella Rehearsals, Tickets Hit $4K Comic's Set Trashing Keegan-Michael Key's Wife Is 'Outlandish, Fabricated,' Sources Say Chix On Netflix -- Guess Whose 'Selling' Hot Shots?! 'Summer House' West Wilson's Alleged Murderer Cousin Dakota Could Face Mental Exam Guess The Future Beauty Pageant Queen This Girl Turned Into! 'White Lotus' Sam Nivola Says Helena Bonham Carter's Sudden Exit Not Shocking Getty Composite Justin Bieber just sent last-minute Coachella ticket prices surging into the thousands of dollars -- and all it took was a few last-minute TikToks of him rehearsing to spark total chaos! Leaked clips of JB running through classics like "Sorry," "One Less Lonely Girl" and "Boyfriend" got fans spiraling, 'cause it wasn't the "Swag" set they were bracing for ... it's set to be a full nostalgia takeover, which has fans scrambling for tickets. Sources also tell TMZ he’s been out there sound-checking all his throwback hits -- basically pulling an "Eras Tour"-style pivot à la Taylor Swift , and going all-in on the classics. Word spread fast, and now it’s a full-on frenzy. Resale prices have exploded in just days ... we’re talking nearly $4K for general admission weekend passes ... for context, they started at $649 back in September! Gallery Launch Gallery Getty Last time Bieber was on tour was in 2022, where he later had to cancel the tour early after he was diagnosed with Ramsay Hunt syndrome ... and with rumors swirling he might not tour again anytime soon, this suddenly feels like a blink-and-you-miss-it Bieber moment -- and looks like fans are all in on paying the price! Concerts Justin Bieber Performs Invite-Only Show in Los Angeles Ahead of Coachella Performance Last night (March 29), the Canadian pop singer played a full set from his two Swag albums, including “Daisies” and “Yukon,” for fans and celebrity friends at The Roxy — marking his first full-length performance in nearly four years. Heather Taylor-Singh Mar 30, 2026 Justin Bieber performs onstage during the 68th GRAMMY Awards at Crypto.com Arena on February 2, 2026 in Los Angeles, California. @grammys (Instagram) Justin Bieber is ramping up for his Coachella headlining performance. Despite the pop-R&B singer being up for six awards at the Juno Awards this weekend, Bieber skipped the Canadian music awards. Instead, he took the stage in the intimate Los Angeles venue, The Roxy, performing a string of Swag songs in anticipation for his Coachella headline performance in April. Up until yesterday morning, there were no public details about the performance. Fans quickly spotted that The Roxy’s marquee sign had been changed to: “Justin Bieber. Tonight Only,” giving passersby a clear hint at the performance taking place that evening. For the intimate concert, the show was invite-only. A handful of Bieber fan accounts on X shared Google Forms for L.A. fans who were able to snag Coachella tickets. Then, a select group of fans were reportedly selected to buy $50 tickets for the show. advertisement There were a handful of celebrity appearances too, including his wife, Hailey Bieber, and their son Jack, former collaborator SZA, country artist Shaboozey, The 1975 frontman Matty Healy and fellow Canadian pop singer Tate McRae. The Calgary-born pop star was also notably absent from the Junos Awards, where she won four awards , including many of the major categories. According to attendees online, the show was supposed to be phone-free — although some footage from the concert has leaked on social media. Bieber exclusively performed hits from his chart-topping album , Swag, released last July, and its successor, Swag II , which came two months later. He kicked off with Swag ’s album opener “All I Can Take,” and alternated between songs from the two albums. “Speed Demon,” “Go Baby” and “Love Song” were just a sampling of the tracks Bieber performed. He then launched into an acoustic set and performed six songs with a stripped-back vibe, including “Mother In You,” “Things You Do” and “Zuma House.” advertisement The “Baby” singer closed out the 25-song set with some of the bigger songs from the Swag albums, playing fan favourite “Devotion,” and Billboard Canadian Hot 100 top 10 hits “Yukon” and “Daisies” in close succession. Taking a page from his stripped-down “Yukon” Grammys performance , the Biebs performed shirtless with no additional bells and whistles. At the end of the show, fans reported that Bieber said: “See you soon.” Possibly a hint to a future tour, or his forthcoming headline set at Coachella, which runs between April 10-12 and April 17-19, 2026. As Bieber gears up for the California festival, it’s set to be a major weekend for the Canadian star, who is claimed to be the highest-paid act in Coachella history. This marks Bieber’s first full performance in nearly four years. He previously took the stage in September 2022 in Brazil during the world tour in support of 2021’s Justice . Due to health struggles, Bieber cancelled the rest of his shows and has only done a handful of one-off performances, including an intimate, invite-only 2024 set attended by Billboard Canada at Toronto’s History for an NHL All-Star Party. Kerry Doole 2h The musically adventurous Calgary music festival Sled Island has just announced its full lineup. New additions include hyperpop rapper Lil Mariko (in her Canadian debut), illuminati hotties , alt-rock group Palehound , Indigenous electronic cello ensemble Cris Derksen Quartet , noisy Midwest slowcore outfit Flooding , Canadian indie rock project Fanclubwallet , mask-wearing thrash punks TEAR DUNGEON , genre-bending post-punk artist J.R.C.G. , New Mexico-based pop-punk band Weedrat , and the final two guest curator selections: experimental musique concrète project Cooling Prongs , and Nigerian‑American pianist Sharon Udoh (who will also be joining fest curators clipping. for their performance at The Palace Theatre on June 20). Emerging acts joining this year’s lineup include award-winning Plains Cree hip-hop artist Drezus , Winnipeg indie rockers Virgo Rising , Whitehorse-based “gateway metal” duo FRANKLIN , ambient pedal steel performer Wayne Patrick Garrett , dance-inspired electronic artist Eejungmi , San Francisco experimental pop project Tricky FM , Toronto-based art rockers Kali Horse , high-energy cow-punk ensemble Spank Williams , post-hardcore powerhouse Midnight Peg , operatic post-punk four-piece Shunk and many, many more. There'll also be comedy acts, a drag brunch, DJ sets and an art exhibit, plus conference panels in the Central Library on June 19. Passes and tickets are on sale now here. title: Justin Bieber’s warmup
subtitle: N/A
content:
Justin Bieber warms up for Coachella with surprise 25-song set at The Roxy Justin Bieber’s warmup Lainey March 30, 2026 – 3 min read Share this post Justin Bieber was nominated for several Juno Awards last night but wasn’t at the event. He had an official reason for it: a surprise invite-only show at The Roxy in LA where he reportedly performed a 25-song set, tracks from SWAG and SWAG II , ahead of his first headlining appearance at Coachella in a couple of weeks. View this post on Instagram A post shared by Rosecrans Media (@rosecransmedia) JB hasn’t performed a full show since 2022, when he cancelled his tour for health reasons. He popped up here and there at Coachella for one-off appearances and also at the Grammys a couple of months ago but there hasn’t been a proper concert in four years – which is why last night might have been a bit of a warmup, and if so, it’s a good decision to do it in a smaller venue, without all kinds of pressure and anticipation, and work out some muscles that haven’t been engaged in so long. I imagine, under the circumstances, this would have been the safest space for him, in the presence of friends and the most hardcore fans, with a few industry types sprinkled in… before going under a huge spotlight, in front of tens of thousands of people, if not more, and livestreamed around the world. Because it’s no secret that Justin hasn’t seemed all that comfortable on tour for a long, long time. Tour life is hard, it’s not for everyone. Even Miley Cyrus has said that she doesn’t want to do it, that it doesn’t line up with her frequency. Coachella, obviously, isn’t the same as a tour, but for someone like Justin, it does come with massive expectations. The fact that he even wants to do it is encouraging, because he’s been off the stage for so long, and now he’s committed to not just five minutes, but closer to two hours. Training and preparation, then, are essential. So now we know he’s not going into it cold, that he at least has a small(er) gig under his belt, he has something to build on. As mentioned, JB went heavy on tracks from SWAG and SWAG II last night – it’ll be interesting to see what older songs, if any, he’ll add to the Coachella set. And perhaps which surprise guests, if any, might join him. Like, I’m not saying he should be doing “Baby”, but “Peaches” with Daniel Caesar and Giveon, for example, I feel like would work with his new material. And also “Holy” off the Justice album. Plus, if he really wants to get the crowd to pop off, going back to Purpose with “Sorry” would pretty much guarantee 100,000 people singing along. Attached - Justin and Hailey Bieber out last weekend in LA. By Trace William Cowen April 9, 2026 Share ADD COMPLEX ON GOOGLE Image via Kevin Winter/The Recording Academy/Getty Images This weekend, Justin Bieber is set to bring SWAG to the Coachella stage. But first, why not add to your physical Bieber collection? SWAG , released in July 2025, is available now on CD and vinyl via Complex. The album, standing as Bieber’s seventh full-length studio effort, arrived as a bit of a surprise at the time. It was followed just two months later by a sequel, SWAG II, again featuring input from Dijon. Below, we break down how to shop SWAG and more via Complex. How to shop Justin Bieber’s SWAG album Last year’s SWAG , which quickly got a sequel, is available on CD and vinyl via Complex. How to shop Justin Bieber’s JUSTICE and other albums JUSTICE , PURPOSE , My World , and more are also available on Complex. JUSTICE , like SWAG , scored an Album of the Year nod at the Grammys . Watch Justin Bieber’s headlining set at Coachella 2026 Bieber’s headlining set, it need not be said, marks a rarity for fans. It also follows in the footsteps of Bieber’s unforgettable 2026 Grammys ceremony performance, meaning the hype is decidedly high. For info on how to watch Coachella from home, click here. Shop more Coachella 2026 Head this way to check out other Coachella merch on Complex. title: Justin Bieber Coachella performance to pave way for greater surprise

content: Justin Bieber Coachella performance to pave way for greater surprise Home latest pakistan world sports showbiz entertainment Royal business health Sci-Tech Food Fact Check big picture Videos shows Watch Entertainment Justin Bieber Coachella performance to pave way for greater surprise Justin Bieber's Coachella set has raised fans' expectations for pop star's comeback By Geo News Digital Desk | Published April 04, 2026 Justin Bieber's Coachella set has raised fans' expectations for pop star's comeback Justin Bieber’s Coachella performance is more than just a comeback, as it is expected to be a beginning of a new era for the pop star. The 31-year-old musician started his comeback to music with the surprise release of his album, SWAG , and then SWAG 2 , after a long hiatus since 2022. The Baby hitmaker will be headlining both the Saturdays in the music festival – April 11 and April 18 and it will mark one of his first big performances since he cancelled the tour midway in 2022 because of health issues. “Bieber has something to prove … to himself and to the industry. And Coachella will be a real indicator of how he moves forward. There’s a lot riding on this, no doubt,” an industry insider told Page Six. The Grammy winner also performed at the Grammys this year and sang his newly released song, Yukon, and according to the insider he has been preparing to launch a tour of his own after getting back on stage in front of his audience. During the years Bieber spent away from the spotlight, he suffered through many fallouts with collaborators and friends – like Scooter Braun, speculations about his health as he appeared sickly thin during his rare outings. However, the Daisies singer has returned and the insider noted that the Coachella performance “could be the precursor to a tour.” The source added, “This is a hard business,” adding that the festival this year is “the most in-demand Coachella in a long time … Clearly, this is the launch for a potential comeback tour. This is not a one-and-done situation.” Despite the lack of live performances from Bieber over the years, they noted that “He’s a talented kid…and people only want to see him succeed. It’s always hard when you [were once a teeny hopper and [then] want to be a legitimate music artist. But everybody wants him.” Travis Kelce shares personal checklist for wedding with Taylor Swift Stevie Nicks carries special message for Taylor Swift at Met Gala Billie Eilish gives final verdict on fourth album after Finneas' hint Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce caught up in 'fake' scenario before wedding Jack Antonoff shares origin story behind Taylor Swift collaborations Kim Kardashian makes major career switch after Broadway premiere Margot Robbie's minimalist Met Gala 2026 dress is not so minimalistic Travis Kelce shares how Taylor Swift transforms him into 'different man' ‘Lord of the Flies' hits Netflix: What sets it apart from previous versions? title: How to Watch Coachella at Home for Free
subtitle: N/A
content: Add TODAY to Google If you can’t make it to Coachella, don’t worry — Coachella can come to you. The 25th edition of the famed Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival, which takes place at the Empire Polo Club in Indio, California, kicks off April 10. A wide range of stars will perform, including Justin Bieber, Sabrina Carpenter, Teddy Swims and Karol G. The event runs over two weekends. The first is April 10, 11 and 12, followed by April 17, 18 and 19. This year’s event will also be available to watch at home, so if you don’t plan to trek to the desert, you can slip into your jammies, fire up your computer or phone and still take in what the festival’s musical acts have to offer. How to Watch Coachella At Home For Free The festival’s performances will be available to stream on YouTube . Head to Coachella’s page and scroll to the stage you’d like to watch, or click here for a playlist of all the available livestreams, which are set to start at 4 p.m. PT on April 10. In addition to the main stage, or the Coachella Stage, shows will be held on seven other stages: Outdoor Theatre, Sahara, Mojave, Gobi, Sonora and Quasar. Here’s the Full Coachella Schedule There are some real heavyweights who will take the main Coachella stage. (All times are PT.) Sabrina Carpenter will perform from 9:05 p.m. until 10:35 p.m. on April 10. She will be followed at midnight by electronic artist Anyma. Teddy Swims will perform earlier in the night at 5:30 p.m. Bieber will headline on April 11 with an 11:25 p.m. show. That will wrap up a night that features performances from other acts such as The Strokes at 9 p.m. and Addison Rae at 5:30 p.m. Recommended Deals Deals What Will Be on Sale This Memorial Day? What To Know, Plus Early Deals To Shop Shop TODAY Travel Awards Shop TODAY Travel Awards 32 Award-Winning Travel Picks Our Editors Love — All on Amazon The final night on April 12 will culminate with Karol G’s 9:55 p.m. set. Young Thug will perform before her at 7:50 p.m. The whole livestream schedule is available here . You can check the entire Coachella schedule for a list of all the talent performing, as well as what stage they’ll be on and what time their sets begin. More highlights include Katseye at 8 p.m. Friday on the Sahara stage, Sombr at 7:05 p.m. Saturday at the Outdoor Theatre, FKA Twigs at 8:45 p.m. Sunday on the Mojave stage and more. The schedule for the second weekend is not yet available. Drew Weisholtz Drew Weisholtz is a reporter for TODAY Digital, focusing on pop culture, nostalgia and trending stories. He has seen every episode of “Saved by the Bell” at least 50 times, longs to perfect the crane kick from “The Karate Kid” and performs stand-up comedy, while also cheering on the New York Yankees and New York Giants. A graduate of Rutgers University, he is the married father of two kids who believe he is ridiculous. Add TODAY to Google

title: Get Coachella tickets now to see Sabrina Carpenter, Justin Bieber + more

content: How to buy Coachella 2026 tickets, Sabrina Carpenter, Justin Bieber Skip to main content Home News Sports Entertainment Lifestyle Opinion Paris Express Press Argus-Courier Booneville Democrat Charleston Express Business Obituaries Crosswords Comics eNewspaper For Subscribers Contributor Content Get Coachella tickets now to see Sabrina Carpenter, Justin Bieber + more Get Coachella tickets now to see Sabrina Carpenter, Justin Bieber + more Trisha Easto USA TODAY The 2026 Coachella Music Festival is officially on the horizon, and the hype is real. This massive concert usually kicks off music festival season in the California desert , and this year’s lineup is nothing short of legendary, with Justin Bieber , Sabrina Carpenter and Karol G set to take the stage. Coachella will take place from April 10-12 and then again from April 17-19 . The road to the desert began in Fall 2025 when the lineup first dropped, followed by the official ticket release on Friday, September 19 . While fans can still find official passes through AXS , secondary options are also available via StubHub . While tickets are still available , they are moving at record speed. If you’re planning to make the trek to Indio, the clock is ticking—grab your passes now before they're gone for good! Get Coachella tickets now Shop Coachella Tickets As of publication, Coachella tickets are on sale and available. The cheapest tickets are starting at $1,310 . Plus, shuttle passes and camping passes are also still available. When you use our sites and apps, we use Cookies Cookies (including similar technologies such as web storage) allow the operators of websites and apps to store and read information from your device. Learn more in our cookie policy . cookies to: provide our sites and apps to you authenticate users, apply security measures, and prevent spam and abuse, and Measurement We count the number of visitors to our pages, the type of device they use (iOS or Android), the browser they use and the duration of their visit to our websites and apps. This data is collected in aggregate and is not tied to specific users. measure your use of our sites and apps Your privacy choices If you click ' Accept all ', we and our partners , including 249 who are part of the IAB Transparency & Consent Framework, will also store and / or access information on a device (in other words, use cookies) and use precise geolocation data and other personal data such as Technical identifiers Technical identifiers are system-generated strings of letters and numbers that can identify your device or you as a user. They include browser cookies, device IDs and your IP address. They may be derived from hashed and / or encrypted email addresses or the statistical matching of other identifiers. technical identifiers and browsing and search data, for analytics, personalised advertising and content, advertising and content measurement, and audience research and services development. If you do not want us and our partners to use cookies and personal data for these additional purposes, click ' Reject all '. With headliners Justin Bieber , Sabrina Carpenter , and Karol G leading the charge of unforgettable performances, this year’s star-studded festival—spanning across the weekends of April 10 and April 17—brought Hollywood’s biggest stars out to Indio, Calif., to enjoy a weekend of music in the desert. But that’s not all. Celebs, both onstage and in the crowds, were dressed for the occasion. Take Kylie Jenner , for instance, who rocked an oversized tank top baring Justin's face as she arrived in the desert. She completed the look with a floral manicure nodding to the singer 's 2025 single "Daisies," writing on Instagram Story, “It’s giving Bieberchella.” And who could ignore Alix Earle ? No stranger to standout festival style, the TikToker got ready for a day out in the sun with a black bikini top. read Kylie Jenner Debuts Justin Bieber-Inspired Nails Before His Coachella Performance And, of course, fans waited with baited breath to see what Sabrina would wear during her Friday Coachella performance. After all, the “Espresso” hitmaker ever-changing corsets were a highlight of her Short n' Sweet Tour in addition to her feel-good performances that had fans feeling light as a feather. And for the “Please Please Please” singer, the honor of headlining Coachella is a dream—and a manifestation—come true. Kylie Jenner/Instagram The Grammy winner—who previously made her Coachella debut in 2024—has been open about checking a headlining gig at the annual festival off her bucket list. And after she was announced as one of the three names selected to do so, the 26-year-old posted a screenshot of the lineup to her social media commemorating the honor. As she captioned the September 2025 Instagram post , “Woman of my word.” Meanwhile, Sabrina’s fellow headliner Karol G, taking the lead position on Sunday, also expressed her excitement for the career milestone. “Headlining Coachella is literally a dream come true,” she told the Los Angeles Times in December. “I was in Coachella in 2022 and I could feel a before and after in my career so being in Coachella, it’s a dream.” And there are plenty more stars where that came from. For more celeb sightings at Coachella 2026, read on… Kevin Mazur/Getty Images for Coachella Justin Bieber & Billie Eilish One less lonely girl. Music News Justin Bieber Shares Inside Look at His Secret Pre-Coachella Show: ‘See You All Soon’ The star gave fans a preview of his upcoming headlining performance during a one-night-only set at The Roxy Theater in West Hollywood. Annie Harrigan Mar 31, 2026 Justin Bieber performs on stage at the 68th GRAMMY Awards held at the Crypto.com Arena on Feb. 1, 2026, in Los Angeles. Christopher Polk/Billboard If you were not one of the lucky fans who got to see Justin Bieber ‘s secret show in L.A. over the weekend, don’t fret: It was just a taste of what’s to come. On Tuesday (March 31), Bieber shared a compilation of clips to X from his recent one-night-only performance at The Roxy Theater, a 500-capacity venue in West Hollywood, Calif. Alongside the under 90-second sequence, Bieber wrote the caption, “See you all soon.” The video starts with a shirtless Bieber taking a bow on a small stage in what is clearly a packed room. The next clip is a look at the theater’s marquee outside, which simply reads “Justin Bieber / Tonight Only.” The following shows an exclusive T-shirt from the event that says “Justin Bieber Live The Roxy Theater” with the date of the performance. advertisement Then viewers get a taste of the performance Bieber gave to the fans lucky enough to be at the Roxy Theater on Sunday night. The singer performed a series of hits from his 2025 albums Swag and Swag II. For his renditions of his Billboard Hot 100 hits “Speed Demon,” “Yukon,” “Walking Away,” “Go Baby” and No. 2 hit “Daisies,” Bieber traverses the small stage alone, at times shirtless and at others donning a TLC CrazySexyCool World Tour tee. “You guys having fun?” Bieber asks the audience in between songs. The crowd cheers back enthusiastically in response. Though he performs alone for a majority of the video, toward the end Bieber is joined by two guitarists for an acoustic performance of “Everything Hallelujah.” “Well, I just want to say thank you so much for coming tonight,” Bieber says at the end of the montage. “This is so beautiful. It’s a little sneak preview into Coachella which is gonna be so much fun.” Fans who didn’t get into his secret performance at the Roxy Theater will be able to see Bieber headline both Saturday nights — April 11 and April 18 — at this year’s Coachella, either in person or via Coachella’s live YouTube feed. advertisement Watch Bieber’s secret show montage below. By Alex Ocho Updated on April 9, 2026 Share ADD COMPLEX ON GOOGLE Image via Arturo Holmes/Getty Images for Coachella Key Takeaways Coachella 2026 streams for free on the official YouTube channel across both weekends (April 10–12 and 17–19), with multiview letting you watch up to four stages at once and switch audio between overlapping sets. The streams start at 7pm ET and 4pm PT Justin Bieber, Sabrina Carpenter, Justin Bieber, KAROL G, Young Thug, BIGBANG, CLIPSE are the major headliners for this year's event. Coachella Merch from the festival and artists is available at the Complex Shop Coachella 2026 set times are officially out, and even if you're not heading to the desert, there are plenty of ways to tune in from home. The festival's livestream experience is expanding again this year, offering more stages, interactive features, and flexible viewing options so you can catch your favorite artists without missing a beat. How to watch the Coachella 2026 livestream from home You can watch performances via the official Coachella YouTube channel . The livestream runs across both festival weekends, April 10 to 12 and April 17 to 19, giving you access to multiple performances in real time across the festival’s seven stages. One of the biggest features is multiview, which allows you to watch up to four stages at the same time in multiview mode from your TV. While in multiview mode, you can switch audio between feeds so you can follow overlapping sets more easily. You can also use the Coachella livestream app, which is designed to make planning and watching easier. It lets you view the full livestream and replay schedule—synced to your time zone—so you know exactly when to tune in. You can use it to personalize your livestream schedule, set reminders, watch highlights on-demand, and more. Who is performing at Coachella 2026? The lineup includes major global names across both weekends, though it’s important to note this list reflects poster highlights and not every artist is guaranteed to be livestreamed. Friday, April 10 and April 17 includes Sabrina Carpenter, Disclosure, Teddy Swims, KATSEYE, Sexyy Red, Central Cee, and Foster the People. Saturday, April 11 and April 18 features Justin Bieber, The Strokes, Given, Addison Rae, Labrinth, SOMBR, Swae Lee, Taemin, PinkPantheress, Davido, and BIA. Sunday, April 12 and April 19 closes out the festival with KAROL G, Young Thug, BIGBANG, Major Lazer, FKA twigs, and CLIPSE. What should you know about set times and availability? Even though the set times are now available, livestream coverage is selective. The listed artists are pulled from the official poster and are not a guarantee of broadcast. A separate schedule for the YouTube broadcast will be made available on Coachella’s official website ahead of each festival day. Where can I buy official 2026 Coachella merchandise? Fans can get their hands on exclusive festival and artist merchandise at Complex.com Related Stories pop-culture Euphoria Season 3 Premiere Set to Screen at Coachella It will be the first-ever screening of a TV series at the annual music and arts festival. Coachella 2026: A hand-picked guide to the best of the fest | Connecticut Public Skip to main content Expand Search Query Show Search © 2026 Connecticut Public FCC Public Inspection Files: WEDH · WEDN · WEDW · WEDY WEDW-FM · WNPR · WPKT · WRLI-FM Public Files Contact · ATSC 3.0 FAQ Menu Show Search Search Query Donate Connecticut Public Radio / WNPR Here & Now Connecticut Public Radio / WNPR Here & Now Next Up: 3:30 PM Today, Explained 0:00 0:00 Here & Now Connecticut Public Radio / WNPR 0:00 0:00 Available On Air Stations On Air Now Playing WNPR Stream Coachella 2026: A hand-picked guide to the best of the fest NPR | 
                    By Dora Levite , Sheldon Pearce , Anamaria Artemisa Sayre Published April 10, 2026 at 3:57 PM EDT Facebook Twitter LinkedIn Email Arturo Holmes / Getty Images The headliners for the 2026 Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival are Sabrina Carpenter, Justin Bieber and Karol G. But with more than 130 other acts, almost all of them streaming live, the festival offers many different experiences. Coachella is the spring break of the music world: a pair of long weekends in the California desert, featuring over 100 acts across eight stages spanning too many genres to count, from vintage groups mounting reunions to the biggest pop stars on the planet to rising talents with viral hits. Nearly the entire event is streamed live via YouTube , starting Friday afternoon, which makes the prospect of catching more acts easier — you don't have to sprint across the grounds of Indio's Empire Polo Club to make it from one set to the next. But even if you're watching from home, the prospect of mapping your route through the weekend in order to catch the greatest possible collection of live experiences can be overwhelming. To help, three members of NPR Music's team have sifted through the lineup to identify a day-by-day guide. Below, you'll find must-see acts and recommendations to ensure you catch the artists you should prioritize when set times conflict. (Note: All set times listed below are Pacific.) Matt Winkelmeyer / Getty Images / Getty Images FRIDAY Plan by Dora Levite Must see: "Young millionaire, man, I feel like Weezy," says fakemink on his recent EP The Boy who cried Terrified . , a ramp-up to his upcoming album. The 20-year-old London prince of SoundCloud rap has racked up enough well-deserved hype through a steady stream of excellent hyperpop singles and star-studded cosigns (SZA, Drake, Frank Ocean, Ecco2K) to sustain a massive North American tour bookended by Coachella on one side and Lollapalooza on the other. Naturally, fakemink's hype has sparked a slew of online discourse, which has seemingly had the effect of splitting his fan base in two: day-one devotees who insist the rest of the world is late, and new appreciators who feel their precious attention is what brought him to the global sphere. Regardless of where you fall, this is the must-see set of the day — a chance to hear some of his very best music and to figure out, if you even care, where you stand in his fandom. Day plan: The best way to prepare yourself for a day at a music festival is to establish your stage loyalties early. Start with Doom Dave 's DJ set at 1 p.m. at the Sonora stage, then release all your pent-up festival anxiety with a cathartic scream when Las Vegas screamo band Febuary takes over. At 2:10 p.m., I'd watch the Cahuilla Bird Singers and Dancers at the Gobi tent, a Coachella staple for the past few years. At 2:50 p.m., the pop star of the hour, Slayyyter, comes on for her first show with a live band since her excellent new album WOR$T GIRL IN AMERICA . Head back to the Sonora stage at 3:40 p.m. to catch the majority of Wednesday 's set — the North Carolina band released one of the greatest rock albums of 2025 and is a guaranteed stellar live show. After that, things get complicated. Start with Lykke Li on the Outdoor Theatre stage at 5:20 p.m.; last time she played Coachella in 2015, she was a festival highlight, and now with new music on the horizon, she's likely to feed the nostalgia the festival loves and bring some more sparkling pop. Head over to Mojave no later than 5:50 to hopefully see Central Cee close out his set with "Sprinter" (cross your fingers for a Dave cameo). Before Dijon starts at the Outdoor Theatre at 6:40 p.m., you'll have time to see the first bit of CMAT , a fresh face in country-tinged theatrical pop, on the Gobi stage. Next, of course, is fakemink — the buzziest name of the day. 7:20 p.m. on the Gobi stage. From there it all falls into place: Turnstile (8:05 p.m., Outdoor Theatre, bound to be a great energy boost) , Sabrina Carpenter (9:05 p.m., Main stage, every person should see "Manchild" live once in their life), Ethel Cain (10:35 p.m., Mojave tent, the Coachella haunting experience), and finish the night with Blood Orange (11:55 p.m., Mojave — maybe recent collaborator Brendan Yates of Turnstile will skip over from the Outdoor Theatre to join the fun). Frazer Harrison / Getty Images / Getty Images SATURDAY Plan by Sheldon Pearce Must see: There is something thrilling about watching Alex G strap on an accordion mid-song for "June Guitar," from last year's Headlights , during a gig, and that alone might be worth the price of a Coachella ticket. (OK, probably not, but definitely worth seeing on a livestream for free.) The only thing preventing the DIY king turned major-label convert from being the can't-miss performance of Saturday is a last-minute addition: 2025 Rock Hall inductee Jack White , who joins the first weekend as a surprise set at the Mojave tent. He likely won't play "Seven Nation Army" — so what. You don't even really have to like his last few albums to appreciate him live. It's the one forum where his finicky guitar ways always pay off big — he will grab three to four axes, rotate through them across the set, and shred like he's playing to scrape together bus fare out of Indio. Day plan: To get the best Saturday experience, start your stream at 2:40 p.m. with the first 20 minutes of Blondshell 's set at the Outdoor Theatre before flipping over to catch Jack White in the Mojave tent. Stretch your legs, grab a bite, walk the dog, then tap in for Ecca Vandal , a South African-born, Melbourne-raised punk-rock rapper who plays the Sonora stage at 4:20 p.m. Hit Alex G (5:10 p.m., Outdoor stage) and the gripping (and polarizing) band Geese (6:15 p.m., Gobi) back to back. You can opt in or out of best new artist Grammy shortlister Sombr 's 7:05 p.m. set at the Outdoor theatre — maybe you want to see what all the hype is about or maybe you need to step away from the screen for a spell — before embracing the exuberant Afropop pioneer Davido (7:50 p.m., Gobi). In the first major conflict of the day, catch PinkPantheress at 8:55 p.m. in the Mojave tent instead of The Strokes over on the main stage; sure, she's nostalgic for the era the band got famous in, but her time is now, post- Fancy That? and her Alysa Liu cosign. If you're really yearning for post-punk revivalists from NYC's aughts indie scene, have no fear: Interpol is on at Mojave right after. Then stay up late for whatever Swag hijinks Saturday headliner Justin Bieber has planned for the main stage. Matt Winkelmeyer / Getty Images / Getty Images SUNDAY Plan by Anamaria Sayre Must see: My friends: Little Simz . This something-for-everyone artist who retains her own unmistakable flair has graced the desert stage before, appearing in tiny print on the 2019 poster and making a guest appearance with Gorillaz in 2023. On Sunday, the U.K. rapper is back with space to release the full Simz flow on a much larger stage. Simz has always had a gift for taking a live opportunity to hit you over the head with her rapid-fire flow. She unleashes venom with impressive control and is always certain to mix equal parts slam and R&B. In this late afternoon solo slot, she could have an opportunity to fill out her set with a tight live band or maybe sneak in some strings, all the better to represent a sound that gets bigger and brighter with each new record. Day plan: The desert is a marathon, not a sprint. You've made it to Sunday (whether on the ground or virtual) so you're well-versed in pacing. We have to start out slow and maybe a little sad, so first stop is Samia (playing the Mojave tent at 3:15 p.m.), leading directly into Little Simz on the same stage. From there, keep the energy up by hopping over to Clipse (5:15 p.m., Outdoor Theatre) for what's sure to be a performance as gripping as their off-kilter beats. Do a quick flip halfway through to make it over to the Sonora stage by 5:50 p.m. for the last half of Los Retros . It's sure to be sonic whiplash, given that these young romantic crooners bring living room vibes, but it's worth the sprint, and anyway, by this point in the weekend you're a pro at juggling disparate sounds. When that's over, if you wanna lean into the mood shifts and go for one more heart-rate spike via hardcore cleanse, you can just make the last 15 minutes of Suicidal Tendencies back at the Mojave tent. Take a little breather, get some sustenance, and hop back to it for some straight-from-Norway dance floor flair with Röyksopp . If you're watching the live stream, you may have to skip the Norwegian gathering (Yuma stage isn't currently included on the YouTube schedule) and trade it for a bumping party closer to home — Georgia-bred rapper Young Thug on the main stage. Now we're sprinting to the finish: You'll split time at a pair of worthwhile overlapping sets by starting with avant-garde English singer FKA twigs (innovation is twigs' most tried and true mode of being, so there's certain to be something we've never seen before), and (if you can tear yourself away before the end) moving on to catch the end of Chicago's own French Police . Close out the night on the main stage, starting at 9:55 p.m. with the first Latina to ever headline Coachella, la bichota herself, Karol G . Copyright 2026 NPR Federal funding is gone. title: Justin Bieber is headlining Coachella this month: How the pop star is forging a new path

Kaitlin Reilly Apr 10, 2026 5 Justin Bieber is back. For the first time in his nearly two-decade-long career, the artist is headlining Coachella, performing on Saturday, April 11, and Saturday, April 18. The singer, who previously appeared on the Indio, Calif., music festival stage as a guest artist alongside acts like Tems , will reportedly receive a massive $10 million payday — a deal he supposedly negotiated directly with festival promoter Goldenvoice. Bieber’s greatly anticipated Coachella performances come after a series of career highs — and lows. His 2025 two-part album, Swag , received four Grammy nominations and became a critical and commercial success. However, that same year, a series of interactions with paparazzi raised alarms over Bieber’s health — audio from which Bieber included on Swag . (​​“It’s not clocking to you that I’m standing on business, is it? I’m a human being, you’re standing around my car, at the beach,” Bieber says on the appropriately titled track “Standing on Business,” featuring Druski.) Swag came after the “Yukon” artist ended his 2022 Justice tour early . He initially postponed dates, citing exhaustion and a desire to make health a priority. That same year, Bieber was diagnosed with Ramsay Hunt syndrome , which left his face partially paralyzed. The singer also parted ways with longtime manager Scooter Braun in 2023 after Braun stepped back from artist management, further suggesting a crossroads in Bieber’s career. That same year, he sold the rights to his catalog of music released through 2021 to Hipgnosis Songs Capital, reportedly for more than $200 million . But now Coachella could mark a major milestone for the pop star. The father of Jack Blues Bieber has already been giving fans something to look forward to in performances leading up to the main event. Advertisement Justin Bieber played a surprise show in Los Angeles ahead of Coachella Before this month’s music festival, Bieber stepped out for a series of intimate, invite-only performances in Los Angeles. In March, he performed at the Roxy Theatre, before hitting the stage at the Troubadour in April. At both shows, he sang songs exclusively off Swag , like "Daisies," "Yukon," "Go Baby" and "Devotion." Bieber shared videos of the performances on Instagram . Typically, Coachella has performers sign a (somewhat controversial) “radius clause,” which prevents artists from performing in Southern California from the December before Coachella takes place through the May after the festival. The goal of the clause is to ensure that Coachella will benefit from the artist’s placement on its lineup. However, artists can occasionally get around the clause or adjust it in their contract. Justin Bieber and Hailey Bieber at the 68th Grammy Awards. (Gilbert Flores/Billboard via Getty Images) While it’s unclear whether Bieber’s Los Angeles shows offer a preview of his entire Coachella setlist, it’s worth noting that because Bieber sold the rights to his back catalog of music to all songs he released through 2021, he will not receive performance royalties for older tracks. Bieber recently performed at the Grammys Bieber’s small shows in Los Angeles weren’t his only performance ahead of Coachella: He also took the Grammys stage in February, where he stripped down to just shorts and socks to sing his Swag track “Yukon.” His wife, Hailey Bieber, was emotional in the audience as Bieber showed off a back tattoo that was supposedly a portrait of the Rhode Beauty founder . The awards show’s executive producer, Ben Winston, said that Bieber’s rehearsal for the set moved quickly. “He came to the stage, he did it once,” the producer told the podcast Rolling Stone Music Now after the show. “It was brilliant. We are seven minutes into his hour-and-a-half rehearsal. And he goes, ‘How’d it look to you? You happy with it?’ And I was like, ‘Oh, my God. It looked beautiful. I loved it.’ And he went, ‘OK, fantastic. See you Sunday!'” Justin Bieber during the Grammys. (John Shearer/Getty Images for The Recording Academy) The stripped-down number was the first time Bieber performed at the Grammys in four years. In 2022, he was joined by Daniel Caesar and Giveon for a performance of “Peaches,” off his 2021 album, Justice . title: Kylie Jenner Dressed Like the Ultimate Belieber Ahead of Justin Bieber’s Coachella 2026 Performance
subtitle: N/A
content: THE RUNDOWN Kylie Jenner is at Coachella weekend 1 and shared photos of her Justin Bieber-themed look on Friday. The star paired a sparkly tank top that had Bieber’s face on it with black pants and blue Skylrk shoes. The reality star has over a decade of history at the festival. Kylie Jenner has returned to Coachella for another year—and literally dressed to support her longtime friend, Justin Bieber, who is headlining the festival on Saturday night. For her outfit on the festival’s first day, Jenner paired a green tank top with Bieber’s face on it with black pants and blue Skylrk shoes. Skylrk is Bieber’s fashion brand. She posted her full look on Instagram Friday afternoon, captioning it, “chelly day 1!!!!!” She also teased her Bieberchella manicure on her Instagram Story: Instagram Advertisement - Continue Reading Below Instagram Instagram Advertisement - Continue Reading Below On TikTok, she shared more videos of herself getting ready in the look: View full post on TikTok View full post on TikTok Jenner has been close friends with Bieber for years. In 2015, she told ELLE UK , “I’m so close to him because I feel like he’s been through exactly what I’ve been through in a completely different way but very similar. So, he’s just...he just...he gets me, and every time I see him he always sits me down and is like, ‘Are you okay? You’re amazing. You can get through this, just stay true to yourself.’ He helps me a lot.” The star has over a decade of history at Coachella as well. Back in 2017, she spoke to ELLE about how she approached dressing for the occasion. “I think it takes a minute for me [to put an outfit together],” she said. “You’ll start thinking about it early, even if you change your mind 10 minutes before you get dressed. For me, I need to try and find a hair color that I haven’t done before. There’s not much left, but I’m committed to finding new options.” Years later, in 2024, Jenner gave ELLE an update on her style evolution more generally for her November cover story. “I think I’m actually going in the opposite direction [of minimalism],” she said. “I’ve definitely had some moments of quiet luxury, as they would say, but I’m always experimenting. At the root of my authentic style, I think I’m more dark feminine.” She credited the shift to the fact “I haven’t had a baby in a few years. The first part of my twenties was having children, learning what my personal style was and then losing it—not knowing how to dress, gaining 60 pounds for both pregnancies. It took me a year to feel like myself again. At 27, I feel more confident and more like myself than ever.”

content: Kylie Jenner's Justin Bieber, Coachella-Inspired Nails Kylie Jenner Debuts Justin Bieber-Inspired Nails Before His Coachella Performance More Videos 0 seconds of 15 seconds Volume 0% Press shift question mark to access a list of keyboard shortcuts Keyboard Shortcuts Enabled Disabled Shortcuts Open/Close / or ? Play/Pause SPACE Increase Volume ↑ Decrease Volume ↓ Seek Forward → Seek Backward ← Captions On/Off c Fullscreen/Exit Fullscreen f Mute/Unmute m Decrease Caption Size - Increase Caption Size + or = Seek % 0-9 Next Up Hayden Panettiere Says Milo Ventimiglia Breakup Was "Out of Left Field" 01:36 Subtitle Settings Off English Font Color White Font Opacity 100% Font Size 100% Font Family Arial Character Edge None Edge Color Black Background Color Black Background Opacity 50% Window Color Black Window Opacity 0% Reset White Black Red Green Blue Yellow Magenta Cyan 100% 75% 50% 25% 200% 175% 150% 125% 100% 75% 50% Arial Courier Georgia Impact Lucida Console Tahoma Times New Roman Trebuchet MS Verdana None Raised Depressed Uniform Drop Shadow White Black Red Green Blue Yellow Magenta Cyan White Black Red Green Blue Yellow Magenta Cyan 100% 75% 50% 25% 0% White Black Red Green Blue Yellow Magenta Cyan 100% 75% 50% 25% 0% This ad will end in 5 Live 00:09 00:05 00:15 Skip Watch : Kylie Jenner Debuts Justin Bieber-Inspired Nails Before His Coachella Performance Kylie Jenner is counting the daisies on her nails. The Kylie Cosmetics founder shared her love for pal Justin Bieber , who is gearing up to headline the second day of Coachella on April 11. While other fans might wear JB’s merch, Kylie got a multicolor, bedazzled floral nail set reminiscent of the daisy Spotwear patches from Justin’s collab with wife Hailey Bieber ’s beauty brand Rhode. The Khy owner, who is no stranger to the festival , shared on her Instagram Story, “It’s giving Bieberchella.” Later, onboard her private jet en route to Coachella, Kylie asked her besties about the performances they’re most excited for. Some longtime BFFs spotted in the aircraft include Stassie Karanikolaou and Kylie’s former assistant Victoria Villarroel . “I’m Kylie,” the 28-year-old shared in a TikTok on April 9, posing alongside her Birkin bag. “And I’m excited to see Justin.” Meanwhile, Stassie said she's pumped to see Young Thug and Victoria cheered for Karol G , who will be making history as the first Latina headliner at Coachella on April 12. read How Karol G Is Making History as a Coachella 2026 Headliner This festival will mark a first for Justin, too. While the 32-year-old has popped up as a guest for several Coachella performers over the years, he will be making his debut solo performance this year. Ahead of the milestone, the “Baby” singer held an intimate concert at the Troubadour as a warm-up to Coachella on April 4, primarily performing songs from his most recent albums SWAG and SWAG II. Kylie Jenner/Instagram Justin has been working hard on his set, teasing his plans on a Twitch livestream last October and sharing, "I’m gonna do the whole, both [ SWAG ] projects, probably, and just find a way to merge some of the old songs in where I can and, like, I haven’t performed these songs live yet, and that’ll be kind of the first time I really do them all live like that." While the “Yukon” artist expressed some anxiety over potential touring, he shared on the stream that Coachella was a safe space for him. He said, “It soothes the soul.” As fans patiently await this year’s Coachella performances—and potential guests—read on to revisit some of the most iconic surprises from past festivals. Kevin Mazur/Getty Images for Coachella Sabrina Carpenter, Madonna (2026) Twenty years after the Material Girl made her Coahcella debut, she returned to the desert for a surprise performance during the "Espresso" singer's headlining set. However, the "full circle" moment was slightly soured when Madonna revealed that her costume went missing after the show. Kevin Mazur/Getty Images for Coachella Anitta, Snoop Dogg (2022) Anitta brought out the rapper as a surprise guest to perform "Onda Diferente." Kevin Mazur/Getty Images for ABA Harry Styles, Shania Twain (2022) Shania joined Harry onstage for his headlining performance at the 2022 Coachella festival, where they performed her hits "Man! I Feel Like a Woman" and "You're Still the One." Jeff Kravitz/FilmMagic Jay-Z, Beyoncé (2010) The "'03 Bonnie and Clyde" husband-and-wife duo came together onstage for the rapper's Coachella performance in 2010, giving festival attendees a surprise rendition of Jay 's hit "Young Forever." Christopher Polk/Getty Images for Coachella Dr. Dre, Snoop Dogg, Tupac Shakur Hologram (2012) When Dr. Dre and Snoop Dogg hit the Coachella stage in 2012, nothing could have prepared fans for the hologram edition of the late Tupac Shakur (the rapper was murdered in 1996). For months after their show, social media still could not get over seeing the image of the California rapper projected alongside his peers. FilmMagic.com/FilmMagic Calvin Harris, Rihanna (2012) Back in 2012, Calvin Harris had the crowd feeling like they were the only fans in the world when he brought out Rihanna as a surprise guest. During their set, the singer belted out her hit "Where Have You Been" before launching into their collab, "We Found Love." Fans were extra delighted when the two did it all over again in 2016. Karl Walter/Getty Images for Coachella 2 Chainz, Fall Out Boy (2013) Sugar, we're going down swinging! At least, that's all fans could think after Fall Out Boy joined 2 Chainz onstage in 2013. "Not only did I come to save hip hop" the rapper told the crowd at the festival. "I came to save rock n' roll." Kevin Winter/Getty Images for Coachella Chance the Rapper, Justin Bieber (2014) Beliebers everywhere rejoiced when Justin Bieber joined Chance the Rapper as a surprise guest during the musician's set in 2014. The two teamed up to perform their collab, "Confident." Jeff Kravitz/FilmMagic Solange, Beyoncé (2014) We all know that Beyoncé can dance, but she proved that she made the best backup dancer during little sister Solange' s set in 2014. Just four years later, the duo showed off their best moves again for Beychella . Christopher Polk/Getty Images for Coachella Drake, Madonna (2015) Who could've seen this collab coming? When Madonna joined Drake onstage during his 2015 set, nothing else really mattered. After Drake performed his song, "Madonna," he brought his lyrics literally to life when the Material Girl came strolling out. And as showstopping as that combo was, it was their kiss that made headlines for weeks after. Kevin Winter/Getty Images for Coachella Future, Drake (2017) What a time to be alive. Since these two collaborated on an entire mixtape together, it only made sense that Drake would pop in to perform with Future in 2017. Kevin Mazur/Getty Images for Coachella Beyoncé, Solange, Destiny’s Child (2018) Let's face it: Beyoncé's headlining performance already had fans in formation, but the extra surprise came not only when Solange joined her sister onstage, but when the long-awaited Destiny's Child reunion finally happened without warning. Bey's hubby, Jay-Z , managed to make an appearance that weekend, too. Kevin Mazur/Getty Images for AG Ariana Grande, NSYNC (2019) For her 2019 performance, Ariana Grande had fans saying anything but "thank you, next" once *NSYNC reunited as her surprise guest stars (minus Justin Timberlake , who was on tour at the time). The forever-in-our hearts boy band sang "Tearin' Up My Heart" and joined in for a special rendition of Ariana's single "Break Up With Your Girlfriend, I'm Bored." title: Is Justin Bieber Only Going to Play New Songs at Coachella?

content: Is Justin Bieber Only Going to Play New Songs at Coachella? - Hits 96 | WDOD-FM Sections Home Contact Us Set Your Mobile Alarm to Hits 96 The Proximity Playlist Healthy Hits Finally Friday News News Channel 9 Storm Track 9 Contests Win Tickets To Ariana Grande Win Ed Sheeran Tickets For Valentine’s Day! Follow Us Facebook Instagram Twitter Youtube iOS App Android App Personalities Kidd Kraddick Morning Show Middays W/ DJ Kev Gino D XYZ With Erik Zachary Kidd’s Kids Σam Belding At 40 w/ Ryan Seacrest Slow Jams w/ R Dub The Chatt It Up Sportsminute A 96 Second Book Review with The Book & Cover Events The Luck of the Draw Summer Kickoff Party at Whiskey Cowgirl! Running of the Chihuahuas 16: The Photos Advertise With Hits 96 Text/Request Line 423-642-9636 About Connect With Us Is Justin Bieber Only Going to Play New Songs at Coachella? March 31, 2026 Gino Devaney , Justin Bieber poses at the red carpet during the 68th Annual Grammy Awards in Los Angeles, California, U.S., February 1, 2026. REUTERS/Mario Anzuoni The Celebrity Beat… Justin Bieber will headline the Saturday lineups at Coachella this year, on April 11th and the 18th .  But fans of his classic stuff might be disappointed. Justin played a secret, invite-only show at the Roxy in Hollywood on Sunday night.  He played 25 songs, and they were ALL from his 2025 albums “SWAG” and “SWAG 2” . At this point, there’s no indication that this is the setlist he’s bringing to Coachella… but if he meant it as a warmup gig, that doesn’t bode well for people who wanna hear the oldies. Back in 2023, Justin sold his catalog for $200 million .  That doesn’t mean he can’t play his older songs… but it’s possible he’ll use Coachella to spotlight the newer stuff that wasn’t part of that deal. It was supposed to be a camera-free show, but some videos did squeak out.  Check ’em out here .
